message:
fcp-audit: add issuer signer binding to AuditEntry

`AuditEntry` previously carried `actor: String` but no signer key,
no signature, and no issuer attestation. `verify_chain` enforced
hash-linkage integrity but performed no authenticity check, so any
entity with append access could produce a chain claiming any actor
that passed `verify_chain` cleanly.

Add optional `issuer_kid: Option<KeyId>` and
`signature: Option<Ed25519Signature>` fields to `AuditEntry`
(serde-default for backwards compatibility), plus:

  - `AuditEntry::signing_bytes()` — canonical signing transcript
    `AUDIT_ENTRY_SIG_DOMAIN || u32(id_len) || id || u32(kid_len) ||
    kid_bytes`. Commits to the *recomputed* `id` (not the
    producer-supplied field), transitively binding every other
    field that participates in `computed_id`.
  - `AuditEntry::sign(&mut self, &Ed25519SigningKey)` — populates
    `issuer_kid` and `signature` in place. Sets the kid first so it
    participates in the signed transcript.
  - `AuditEntry::verify_signature(&Ed25519VerifyingKey)` — typed
    failure with `AuditError::SignerMissing` (no issuer/signature)
    and `AuditError::SignatureInvalid` (kid mismatch or signature
    fails to verify).
  - `verify_chain_with_signers(entries, head, zone_id, key_lookup)`
    — runs `verify_chain` for integrity AND requires every entry to
    sign-verify against the resolver-supplied key. Adds
    `AuditError::UnknownIssuer` for entries whose `issuer_kid` is
    not in the resolver.

Crate-level rustdoc now explicitly states the authenticity vs
integrity distinction and points unsigned callers at the new
`_with_signers` variant. `verify_chain`'s rustdoc adds a NORMATIVE
"Authenticity gap" callout.

Three new error variants: `SignerMissing { seq }`,
`SignatureInvalid { seq }`, `UnknownIssuer { seq }` — all with
distinct FCP error codes (5015/5016/5017).

Six regression tests cover: sign/verify roundtrip, wrong-key
rejection, post-signing actor mutation rejection, unsigned-entry
rejection, fully-signed multi-entry chain accepted by
`verify_chain_with_signers`, mid-chain unsigned entry rejected
with `SignerMissing` at the offending seq, unknown-issuer
rejection.

(One pre-existing test `chain_head_has_quorum_boundary` fails in
isolation under the current API contract — unrelated to signer
binding; that test asserts `has_quorum()` returns true when
`signature_count = 1` but `signatures.is_empty()`, which the API
intentionally rejects.)

Closes flywheel_connectors-la0l1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
